sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Fomento Economico Mexicano, S.A.B. de C.V. (FMX) Reports Q4 Revenue Up 5.7% YoY
feed.summary: Fomento Económico Mexicano, S.A.B. de C.V. (FMX) reported a Q4 revenue increase of 5.7% year over year (YoY). The company's CEO, Jose Antonio Fernandez Garza-Laguera, highlighted strong performance from OXXO and Coca-Cola FEMSA, with OXXO traffic recovering and Coca-Cola FEMSA achieving consolidated volume growth. FMX also announced a restructuring effort to streamline operations and is optimistic about future growth despite newly implemented taxes in Mexico.

feed.title: Fidelity International High Dividend ETF (NYSE:FIDI) - Share Price and Research
feed.summary: This article provides current share price and historical data for Fidelity International High Dividend ETF (NYSE:FIDI). As of March 4, 2026, FIDI's share price is $27.76, reflecting a 2.60% drop from the previous close. The ETF recorded a 52-week high of $28.84 and a 52-week low of $19.44, with its last dividend payment of $0.25 on December 23, 2025.

The report includes performance metrics for the current year, last year, and financial years, along with a daily share price history table.

It details the fund's key statistics, including its $510.71 million AUM, 3.09% dividend yield, and 0.80% expense ratio. The analysis also covers FEP's sector and regional exposures, top holdings, dividend history, and recent performance metrics.

feed.title: OGE fights for CWIP charges at power plant
feed.summary: Oklahoma Gas and Electric (OG&E) is engaged in a dispute with state organizations over its request to apply the new Construction Work in Progress (CWIP) law for its Horseshoe Lake Units 13 & 14 natural gas generation project. Opponents argue that OG&E's application, filed before the law's effective date, should not qualify, and that customers shouldn't pay for the project before completion. The Oklahoma Corporation Commissioners are set to deliberate on motions to dismiss OG&E's application.

feed.title: RTX Seeks Larger Role in FAA’s $12.5B ATC Overhaul
feed.summary: RTX is actively positioning its AutoTrac platform for the FAA's Brand New Air Traffic Control System (BNATCS) Common Automation Platform (CAP) contract, a major part of the $12.5 billion federal overhaul. The CAP aims to integrate the FAA's existing fragmented en route and terminal air traffic control systems into a single digital interface for improved efficiency and managing increasing air traffic. Given its current role as the primary contractor for the FAA's Standard Terminal Automation Replacement System (STARS) and global experience, RTX is well-suited for this significant contract.

feed.title: The Walt Disney Company Secures up to $10 Billion in Loans Ahead of New CEO Takeover
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company has secured two new credit lines totaling almost $10 billion, weeks before Josh D’Amaro takes over as CEO. These unsecured credit agreements, a $5.25 billion short-term line and a $4 billion long-term line, replace expiring ones and are intended for general operating costs. Notably, these funds cannot be used for FuboTV or specific international theme parks like Shanghai and Hong Kong Disneyland.

feed.title: Popular Chain Restaurant Closing Most Remaining Locations In Coming Weeks
feed.summary: Bahama Breeze, a popular Caribbean-inspired chain restaurant owned by Darden Restaurants Inc., is closing 14 of its locations by April 5, 2026. The remaining 14 locations will be converted into other Darden brands over the next 12-18 months. Darden does not expect these closures and conversions to significantly impact its financial results.

feed.title: CSLLY or EXAS: Which Is the Better Value Stock Right Now?
feed.summary: This article compares CSL Limited Sponsored ADR (CSLLY) and Exact Sciences (EXAS) to determine which offers better value. Utilizing Zacks Rank and Style Scores, it concludes that CSLLY is the superior value option due to its #2 (Buy) Zacks Rank, improving earnings outlook, and more favorable valuation metrics like P/E, PEG, and P/B ratios compared to EXAS.

feed.title: Simon Property Group (SPG) CAO settles RSUs, withholds shares for tax
feed.summary: Simon Property Group's Chief Administrative Officer, John Rulli, recently settled 4,124 restricted stock units (RSUs) into common shares. As part of this transaction, 1,191 common shares were withheld at a price of $203.85 per share to cover tax obligations. Following these movements, Rulli directly owns 38,643 common shares and indirectly holds an additional 2,004 shares through a 401(k) plan.

feed.title: Bull Signal Flashing Amid Hotel Stock Pullback
feed.summary: Shares of Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc (HLT) are showing a bullish signal after pulling back approximately 8% from their February peak. The stock has tested a historically bullish trendline, and similar occurrences in the past have led to average gains of 3.4% one month later. Additionally, options for HLT are currently affordable, with low implied volatility expectations, and a potential unwind of bearish sentiment could support its long-term uptrend.

feed.title: SPG (SPG) accounting chief settles RSUs and withholds shares for tax
feed.summary: Adam Reuille, SVP and Chief Accounting Officer of Simon Property Group, settled 1,031 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) into common stock on March 2, 2026. To cover tax obligations, 336 common shares were disposed of at $203.85 per share. Following these transactions, Reuille directly held 17,011 common shares and indirectly held 928 shares through a 401(k) plan.

feed.title: Simon Property Group (NYSE: SPG) CFO converts 5,155 RSUs into common stock
feed.summary: Brian J. McDade, CFO of Simon Property Group (NYSE: SPG), converted 5,155 restricted stock units (RSUs) into common stock on March 2, 2026. This transaction increased his direct holdings to 47,151 common shares, with an additional 410 shares held indirectly through a 401(k) plan. The conversion was made at no exercise price as part of an RSU settlement upon vesting.

feed.title: Is Procter & Gamble (PG) Offering Value After Recent Share Price Swings?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Procter & Gamble's (PG) valuation using two methods: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) and Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio. The DCF model suggests PG is undervalued by 21.7% with an intrinsic value of $204 per share, while its P/E ratio of 23.0x is below its proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 29.0x, also indicating undervaluation. The article also presents different investor narratives for PG, with bull and bear cases yielding fair values of US$168.00 and US$121.06 respectively, highlighting the subjective nature of valuation.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider NRG Energy (NRG) After Its Strong Multi Year Rally?
feed.summary: NRG Energy has seen significant multi-year gains, prompting investors to question its current valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 70.1% at $162 per share, with an intrinsic value of $541.29. However, its P/E ratio of 43.65x is higher than the industry average, indicating it might be overvalued based on earnings.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share worth $522
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a ten percent owner of Texas Pacific Land Corp (TPL), recently purchased a single share of TPL stock for $522. The purchase occurred while TPL is trading near its 52-week high, with significant year-to-date gains. Despite the optimistic price action, an InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock might be overvalued.

feed.title: Ross Stores' ambitious targets are achievable given standout quarter -- analysts (ROST:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Ross Stores (ROST) exceeded expectations in its recent quarter, surpassing top- and bottom-line estimates and issuing strong guidance. Analysts believe the company's ambitious targets are attainable, attributing this performance to robust comparable store sales, a renewed merchandise selection, and strategic price management despite tariff challenges. Despite potential risks from distribution center investments and opportunistic buying, Ross Stores' effective handling of market challenges and consistent growth instill confidence among analysts.

feed.title: DOJ Investigates Major Fertilizer Producers Over Possible Price Coordination
feed.summary: The US Department of Justice has launched an investigation into several large fertilizer manufacturers, including Nutrien Ltd., Mosaic Co., and CF Industries Holdings Inc., over potential price coordination to inflate prices in the American market. This scrutiny arises amidst existing concerns about consolidation in the fertilizer industry and historically high prices, despite recent declines from peak levels. The probe is in its early stages, focusing on whether pricing strategies violate US antitrust law, though no accusations of wrongdoing have been made yet.

feed.title: National Vision Holdings, Inc. 2025 Q4 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation (NASDAQ:EYE) 2026-03-04
feed.summary: This article presents the slide deck published by National Vision Holdings, Inc. in conjunction with their Q4 2025 earnings call. The document is provided by SA Transcripts, Seeking Alpha's team dedicated to developing transcript-related projects, including thousands of quarterly earnings calls.

feed.title: Tesla boosted Elon Musk's wealth and U.S. inequality, analysis argues
feed.summary: Oxfam analysis claims Tesla's pay structure, tax practices, and political influence have contributed to Elon Musk's wealth and increased U.S. inequality. The report highlights Musk's vastly higher pay compared to employees, the company's use of tax havens, and alleged lack of corporate oversight. Oxfam advocates for increased corporate transparency and reforms to address such wealth disparities.

feed.title: Barclays Raises Public Storage Price Target to $347 From $331
feed.summary: Barclays has increased its price target for Public Storage (PSA) to $347, up from the previous $331. This update reflects recent financial news for the company, including its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings results, dividend declarations, and executive changes.

feed.title: Simon Property Group (NYSE: SPG) GC settles RSUs, withholds 1,024 shares for taxes
feed.summary: Steven E. Fivel, General Counsel for Simon Property Group (NYSE: SPG), settled 4,124 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) on March 2, 2026, receiving an equivalent number of common shares. A total of 1,024 shares were subsequently withheld at $203.85 per share to cover tax obligations related to the vesting. Following these transactions, Fivel directly holds 42,034 common shares, with an additional 383 shares held indirectly by his spouse.

feed.title: Sempra Experiences Evaluation Revision Amid Strong Market Performance and Positive Indicators
feed.summary: Sempra, a key player in the power sector, has undergone an evaluation revision due to strong market performance, significantly outperforming the S&P 500 over the past year with a 30.68% return. While technical indicators like MACD and Bollinger Bands show bullish trends, suggesting a positive outlook, the KST indicates a mildly bearish trend on a weekly basis, warranting attention. The company's stock is currently priced at $95.20, having reached a 52-week high of $97.43, reflecting its resilience in a competitive market.

feed.title: Teamsters: ‘Kroger has declared open war’ on union workers
feed.summary: The Teamsters union alleges that Kroger is planning a "calculated shift" towards a Walmart-like model of outsourcing and subcontracting in its labor practices, particularly under its new CEO, former Walmart executive Greg Foran. The union claims this strategy will involve replacing unionized workers with lower-wage, non-union labor, which they view as a declaration of "open war" on their members. Despite the union's concerns, industry analysts consulted by Supermarket News describe Foran as an executive who has previously prioritized workers and even raised pay for store-level employees at Walmart.

feed.title: San José State University and Teradyne Partner to Advance Engineering Education and Workforce Readiness
feed.summary: San José State University (SJSU) has partnered with Teradyne, Inc. to enhance engineering education, particularly in memory test engineering. This two-year initiative will provide SJSU's Charles W. Davidson College of Engineering with financial support, industry expertise, and advanced test equipment, preparing students for the semiconductor industry. A new Electrical Engineering elective, developed in collaboration with Teradyne engineers, will offer hands-on skills using industry-standard tools, alongside support for student projects, organizations, conferences, and career development.

feed.title: Microsoft to pay full share of property taxes for LaPorte data center project
feed.summary: Microsoft has agreed to pay its full share of property taxes for its LaPorte data center project, significantly benefiting the LaPorte Community School Corporation. This new agreement replaces a previous deal that offered Microsoft a 100-percent abatement on personal property taxes. The decision follows Microsoft's announcement in January that it would no longer seek local tax breaks for new projects.

feed.title: AT&T (NYSE: T) withdraws 0.250% Global Notes due 2026 from NYSE
feed.summary: AT&T Inc. filed a Form 25 with the SEC, notifying the New York Stock Exchange LLC of the removal of its 0.250% Global Notes due 2026 from listing and registration. This action signifies a voluntary withdrawal by AT&T, with both the Exchange and the issuer confirming compliance with the relevant rules. The filing does not provide any business rationale for the withdrawal, focusing solely on the procedural aspects of delisting.

feed.title: Abbott Labs to face trial on infant formula
feed.summary: Abbott Laboratories is facing a trial in Chicago over lawsuits claiming its baby formula caused necrotising enterocolitis (NEC) in premature infants. Four families allege Abbott failed to warn about the risks associated with its cow's milk-based formula, while Abbott maintains its products are essential for premature babies and do not cause NEC. This trial is one of nearly 1,000 similar lawsuits filed against Abbott and Mead Johnson.

feed.title: CVS Pharmacy is quietly changing how you get meds in the US
feed.summary: CVS Pharmacy is evolving beyond a simple drugstore to become a comprehensive healthcare hub, offering services like same-day prescription delivery, walk-in clinics, and virtual care. This strategic shift by CVS Health aims to make CVS the primary point of contact for everyday health needs, integrating digital access, in-store care, and prescription cost management. While offering significant convenience and accessibility, this expansion also raises questions about care fragmentation, data privacy, and market power concentration within the US healthcare landscape.

feed.title: Jefferies and HSBC Cut Adobe Inc. (ADBE) Price Targets
feed.summary: Jefferies and HSBC have both lowered their price targets for Adobe Inc. (ADBE), citing competitive risks from AI tools and broader challenges in the application software sector. Jefferies revised its price target from $400 to $290, maintaining a Hold rating, while HSBC reduced its target from $388 to $302, also keeping a Hold rating. Both firms believe that AI-powered tools could impact Adobe's core creative business and overall software performance.

feed.title: Teradyne (TER) NASDAQ down 7.21% Mar 04 2026: AI demand and outlook
feed.summary: Teradyne (TER) stock dropped 7.21% on March 4, 2026, driven by profit-taking despite strong Q4 results and guidance linked to AI datacenter chip demand. While the company exhibits robust fundamentals and high analyst sentiment, its valuation is premium, with a trailing PE of 87.92. Meyka AI's forecast model suggests potential downside risk, advising investors to consider position sizing and monitor order trends due to high risk exposure to AI capital expenditure.

feed.title: Melius Research and Stifel Downgrade Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) to Hold
feed.summary: Melius Research and Stifel have both downgraded Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) to Hold, citing concerns about pressure on its 365 business from AI and the need for sharply increased capital expenditures to compete with other tech giants. Stifel specifically noted worries about "too optimistic" revenue and earnings expectations for fiscal year 2027, along with supply constraints in Azure and rising AI competition. These downgrades suggest potential challenges for Microsoft despite its strong market position.

feed.title: PayPal Joins Forces With TCS Blockchain for Faster Invoice Settlements
feed.summary: PayPal is collaborating with TCS Blockchain to accelerate invoice settlements for trucking and transportation companies. Utilizing PayPal USD (PYUSD) stablecoin and blockchain-based digital assets, this partnership aims to reduce settlement times and costs for carriers, addressing long-standing cash flow issues in the supply chain industry. The solution offers significantly cheaper and faster settlements compared to traditional factoring, with same-day settlement capabilities and full transparency.

feed.title: Trump Bought Netflix Debt Amid Paramount’s Fight for Warner Bros.
feed.summary: New financial disclosures reveal that President Donald Trump acquired a significant amount of Netflix bonds in January, following an earlier purchase in December. These acquisitions occurred amidst Paramount's efforts to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery, a deal Netflix was also reportedly pursuing. Despite the White House's statement that Trump's investments are managed by independent managers to replicate market indexes, the timing of these Netflix bond purchases adds intrigue given Netflix's subsequent decision not to raise its bid for Warner Bros. Discovery after a planned meeting between its CEO and Trump was canceled.

feed.title: Billionaire Investor Cuts Amazon And Starts Fresh With Alibaba
feed.summary: Billionaire investor Daniel Loeb, through his hedge fund Third Point LLC, reduced his stake in Amazon.com Inc (NASDAQ: AMZN) and initiated a new position in Alibaba Group Holding Ltd (NYSE: BABA) during the fourth quarter of FY25. The article details recent key events for both companies, provides a technical analysis of Alibaba, and outlines its upcoming earnings and analyst outlook, noting a "Buy" rating with an average price target of $188.69.

feed.title: Apple unveils MacBook Neo, its most affordable model yet
feed.summary: Apple is set to release its most affordable laptop, the MacBook Neo, on March 11, priced at about $600. The new model will feature a chip similar to the iPhone 16 Pro, but with a smaller screen and less memory than other MacBooks. This launch follows Apple's recent announcement of a lower-priced iPhone 17, aiming to attract budget-conscious consumers.

feed.title: NTRS (NASDAQ: NTRS) Form 144 shows proposed 3,255-share sale via Fidelity
feed.summary: A Form 144 filing indicates a proposed sale of 3,255 common shares of Northern Trust (NASDAQ: NTRS) through Fidelity Brokerage Services with a sale date of March 4, 2026. These shares originated from Restricted Stock Vesting on March 1, 2026, as compensation. The filing also notes a separate, completed sale of 1,450 common shares by David W. Fox Jr. on February 25, 2026, for $208,872.50.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics (TPL) logs small Texas Pacific Land share purchase
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a significant owner of Texas Pacific Land Corp (TPL), reported buying 1 share of common stock for $522.34 in an open-market purchase. Following this transaction on March 3, 2026, Horizon Kinetics directly holds 3,469,107 shares in TPL. The report also clarified a previous Schedule 13D amendment regarding beneficial ownership and confirmed that Murray Stahl does not exercise investment discretion over the issuer's securities, with all figures adjusted for a three-for-one stock split effective December 22, 2025.

feed.title: EFFE Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:EFFE
feed.summary: The article provides a detailed overview of the Harbor Osmosis Emerging Markets Resource Efficient ETF (EFFE), including its current price, NAV, AUM, and performance metrics. It describes the fund's active investment strategy, which targets resource-efficient companies in emerging markets based on their carbon emissions, water use, and waste generation. The fund aims to invest in 70-90 securities, avoiding certain industries, and rebalances monthly.

feed.title: FIF - FT Energy Income Partners Enhanced Income ETF Latest Stock News & Market Updates
feed.summary: This page provides news and analysis for FT Energy Income Partners Enhanced Income ETF (FIF), including summaries from Rhea-AI about the impact and sentiment of each news release. It details recent distribution notices from the First Trust Energy Infrastructure Fund for various months in 2024. The page also offers stock data such as market capitalization and links to educational guides and market tools.

feed.title: Shareholder Alert: The Ademi Firm investigates whether Farmer Brothers Coffee Co. is obtaining a Fair Price for its Public Shareholders – Company Announcement
feed.summary: Ademi LLP is investigating Farmer Brothers (NASDAQ: FARM) for potential breaches of fiduciary duty related to its transaction with Royal Cup Coffee and Tea, where shareholders will receive $1.29 per share in cash. The firm is concerned about a significant penalty clause for competing bids and whether the board is fulfilling its duties to all shareholders. They specialize in shareholder litigation for buyouts, mergers, and individual shareholder rights.

feed.title: ‘Magnificent Seven’ stocks mostly climb, with big gains for Amazon and Tesla
feed.summary: The "Magnificent Seven" big tech stocks mostly rose on Wednesday afternoon, with Amazon and Tesla leading the gains. The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF saw a 1.7% increase, propelled by Amazon's 3.3% rise and Tesla's nearly 3% jump. Other stocks in the ETF, except for Alphabet and Apple which traded flat, also posted sharp gains.

feed.title: |‘Magnificent Seven’ stocks mostly climb, with big gains for Amazon and Tesla
feed.summary: |The "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks mostly saw gains on Wednesday, with Amazon and Tesla leading the way with significant climbs. The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF, which holds these stocks, was up 1.7%. This performance contributed to a broader market recovery with the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq all bouncing back.

feed.title: Should FDA Fast Track Status for Nipocalimab in Lupus Reshape Johnson & Johnson’s (JNJ) Immunology Ambitions?
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson received FDA Fast Track designation for nipocalimab to treat systemic lupus erythematosus, underscoring the importance of its FcRn-blocking platform in autoantibody-driven diseases. This development strengthens J&J's pipeline as it navigates biosimilar pressures on STELARA and ongoing legal exposures. While encouraging for future revenue, investors must still consider broader market pressures and the company's financial forecast, including a projected 4.7% yearly revenue growth to $104.1 billion by 2028.

feed.title: Henry Schein Conference Appearances Spotlight Valuation Upside And Debt Risk
feed.summary: Henry Schein is scheduled to present at Leerink’s Global Healthcare Conference and Barclays 28th Annual Global Healthcare Conference in March, aiming to engage investors and discuss its positioning in the healthcare market. The stock is currently trading about 11% below analyst targets and is estimated to be 52.6% undervalued by Simply Wall St. Investors are advised to watch for commentary on growth, capital allocation, technology investments, and debt management during these conferences, as the company carries a flagged risk related to a high level of debt.

feed.title: AT&T Shares Rise 9.5% in a Year: Should You Invest Now?
feed.summary: AT&T (T) has seen a 9.5% rise in its shares over the past year, outperforming the Wireless National industry but lagging behind the broader tech sector and S&P 500. The company shows strong momentum in its Communications segment, driven by customer additions, a successful convergence strategy, and technological advancements like AI-native network architecture. However, AT&T faces challenges including spectrum crunch in a saturated market, high long-term debt, and stiff competition, leading Zacks to rate it as a #3 (Hold) stock.

feed.title: Lulus Shares Jump 66% in a Year: Should You Buy the Stock?
feed.summary: Lulus Fashion Lounge Holdings, Inc. (LVLU) shares have significantly outperformed the industry, surging 65.6% over the past year. This growth is driven by strong digital brand positioning, data-driven merchandising, improved product margins, and expanding wholesale partnerships, particularly in the occasion wear segment. Despite facing macroeconomic headwinds and operating losses, the company is considered undervalued compared to industry averages, presenting a potential investment opportunity.
